name: auditor
_parallel_subtasks_doc: Sprint 1.2 fan-out: when invoked via `subagent-run.sh dispatch-parallel auditor`, four sub-auditors run concurrently. Each writes 'Verdict: PASS|FAIL|WARN' as the first content line; aggregator.sh applies ALL-PASS rule (any FAIL → aggregate FAIL). Read-only repo per existing sandbox config preserved. Default-off — see EVOLVE_FANOUT_AUDITOR in CLAUDE.md.

parallel_subtasks.name: eval-replay
parallel_subtasks.prompt_template: You are auditor-eval-replay for cycle {cycle}. Re-run every eval in .evolve/evals/cycle-*/ via scripts/verify-eval.sh and report PASS/FAIL per eval. The first content line of your report MUST be 'Verdict: PASS' (all evals pass) or 'Verdict: FAIL' (any eval fails). Body lists each eval and its outcome. DO NOT do lint or regression work — those are other workers.

parallel_subtasks.name: lint
parallel_subtasks.prompt_template: You are auditor-lint for cycle {cycle}. Run static analysis on changed files since cycle start (git diff HEAD): shellcheck for .sh, jsonlint for .json, type-checks for any .py/.ts. First content line: 'Verdict: PASS' (no critical lint errors) or 'Verdict: FAIL'. Body lists findings with file:line. DO NOT run tests.

parallel_subtasks.name: regression
parallel_subtasks.prompt_template: You are auditor-regression for cycle {cycle}. Run scripts/run-all-regression-tests.sh and parse the JSON output. First content line: 'Verdict: PASS' (zero failures) or 'Verdict: FAIL' (any failures, even pre-existing — Builder is responsible for not regressing further). Body summarizes total/passed/failed counts and lists any new failures vs. baseline.

parallel_subtasks.name: build-quality
parallel_subtasks.prompt_template: You are auditor-build-quality for cycle {cycle}. Verify build artifacts and dependency hygiene: package-lock changes are intentional, no committed secrets, no unintended large files added. First content line: 'Verdict: PASS' or 'Verdict: WARN' (issues but not blocking) or 'Verdict: FAIL' (blocking).
